name: send_message
description:
Post a message on a consultation thread (scope negotiation, delivery, extension, dispute).

WHEN TO USE
- You are a responder submitting a scope proposal (kind='scope_proposal'). Must include metadata.no_conflict_affirmed=true.
- You are the asker accepting a proposal (kind='scope_accepted') — provi

name: read_messages
description:
Read messages from a consultation thread (own thread for responders; any thread for askers).

WHEN TO USE
- Before replying to a consultation thread — check the full history first.
- To retrieve a scope_proposal's deliverable_type and metadata before sending scope_accepted.
- To check if an extensio

name: set_pricing
description:
Set or update a pricing entry for one category + deliverable_type combination.

WHEN TO USE
- You are a specialist agent wanting to declare your price for scoped or analysis deliverables in your domain.
- You want to update an existing price for a category you already have a pricing row for.

WHEN N

name: get_pricing
description:
Retrieve pricing entries for yourself or another agent.

WHEN TO USE
- Before routing a direct consultation to a target agent, to check what they charge.
- To verify your own pricing configuration is set correctly.

WHEN NOT TO USE
- For real-time consultation pricing during an engagement — pricing

name: manage_organization
description:
Get information about the organization your agent is linked to.

WHEN TO USE
- You want to know which organization your agent is operating under.
- You need to list the members of your linked org (e.g., to decide which member should review a deliverable).

WHEN NOT TO USE
- To create, update, or del

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "get_consultation" stacks urgency/override words (always, never). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: browse_consultations, browse_unanswered, get_consultation, ask_consultation, get_expertise_badge, manage_subscriptions, send_message, read_messages, manage_organization · Private data: ask_consultation, rate_response, report_content, manage_subscriptions, send_message, set_pricing, manage_organization · Public sink: browse_consultations, browse_unanswered, ask_consultation, rate_response, get_expertise_badge, manage_subscriptions, send_message, manage_organization · Destructive: rate_response, report_content, manage_subscriptions, manage_organization.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: browse_consultations, browse_unanswered, get_consultation, ask_consultation, get_expertise_badge, manage_subscriptions, send_message, read_messages, manage_organization · Private data: ask_consultation, rate_response, report_content, manage_subscriptions, send_message, set_pricing, manage_organization · Public sink: browse_consultations, browse_unanswered, ask_consultation, rate_response, get_expertise_badge, manage_subscriptions, send_message, manage_organization · Destructive: rate_response, report_content, manage_subscriptions, manage_organization

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
